initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates for nonzero rows / columns
rowHasNonZero(R) :- input(R, _, C), color(R, _, V), V != 0.
colHasNonZero(C) :- input(_, C, V), V != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Bounding box of the nonzero cells
minRow = #min{ R : rowHasNonZero(R) }.
maxRow = #max{ R : rowHasNonZero(R) }.
minCol = #min{ C : colHasNonZero(C) }.
maxCol = #max{ C : colHasNonZero(C) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 44 blocks (the pattern blocks)
first_block(R,C)  :- row(R), col(C),
                     R >= minRow, R <= minRow + 3,
                     C >= minCol, C <= minCol + 3.
second_block(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),
                     R >= maxRow + 3, R <= maxRow + 6,
                     C >= maxCol + 3, C <= maxCol + 6.

% Block predicates used by the generic rules
block1(R,C) :- first_block(R,C).
block2(R,C) :- second_block(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Choose the most frequent colour (the mode)
1 { mode(M) : color(M) } 1 :- M != 0.

% Count occurrences of each colour 1..9
count(1) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,1) }.
count(2) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,2) }.
count(3) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,3) }.
count(4) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,4) }.
count(5) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,5) }.
count(6) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,6) }.
count(7) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,7) }.
count(8) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,8) }.
count(9) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,9) }.

% Enforce that the chosen colour is a colour of maximal frequency
% (no other colour has a strictly larger count)
:- not mode(M), count(C), count(M), count(C) > count(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output cells for the two pattern blocks
1 { output(R,C,Color) : M = Color } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), mode(M), first_block(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Color) : M = Color } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), mode(M), second_block(R,C).

% Output for cells that are not in any block (they must be 0)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : Color = 0 } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), not block1(R,C), not block2(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Basic output cell definition
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates for nonzero rows / columns
rowHasNonZero(R) :- input(R, _, C), color(R, _, V), V != 0.
colHasNonZero(C) :- input(_, C, V), V != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Bounding box of the nonzero cells
minRow = #min{ R : rowHasNonZero(R) }.
maxRow = #max{ R : rowHasNonZero(R) }.
minCol = #min{ C : colHasNonZero(C) }.
maxCol = #max{ C : colHasNonZero(C) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 44 blocks (the pattern blocks)
first_block(R,C)  :- row(R), col(C),
                     R >= minRow, R <= minRow + 3,
                     C >= minCol, C <= minCol + 3.
second_block(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),
                     R >= maxRow + 3, R <= maxRow + 6,
                     C >= maxCol + 3, C <= maxCol + 6.

% Block predicates used by the generic rules
block1(R,C) :- first_block(R,C).
block2(R,C) :- second_block(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Choose the most frequent colour (the mode)
1 { mode(M) : color(M) } 1 :- M != 0.

% Count occurrences of each colour 1..9
count(1) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,1) }.
count(2) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,2) }.
count(3) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,3) }.
count(4) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,4) }.
count(5) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,5) }.
count(6) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,6) }.
count(7) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,7) }.
count(8) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,8) }.
count(9) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,9) }.

% Enforce that the chosen colour is a colour of maximal frequency
% (no other colour has a strictly larger count)
:- not mode(M), count(C), count(M), count(C) > count(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output cells for the two pattern blocks
1 { output(R,C,Color) : M = Color } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), mode(M), first_block(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Color) : M = Color } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), mode(M), second_block(R,C).

% Output for cells that are not in any block (they must be 0)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : Color = 0 } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), not block1(R,C), not block2(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Basic output cell definition
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.

final_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates for nonzero rows / columns
rowHasNonZero(R) :- input(R, _, C), color(R, _, V), V != 0.
colHasNonZero(C) :- input(_, C, V), V != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Bounding box of the nonzero cells
minRow = #min{ R : rowHasNonZero(R) }.
maxRow = #max{ R : rowHasNonZero(R) }.
minCol = #min{ C : colHasNonZero(C) }.
maxCol = #max{ C : colHasNonZero(C) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 44 blocks (the pattern blocks)
first_block(R,C)  :- row(R), col(C),
                     R >= minRow, R <= minRow + 3,
                     C >= minCol, C <= minCol + 3.
second_block(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),
                     R >= maxRow + 3, R <= maxRow + 6,
                     C >= maxCol + 3, C <= maxCol + 6.

% Block predicates used by the generic rules
block1(R,C) :- first_block(R,C).
block2(R,C) :- second_block(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Choose the most frequent colour (the mode)
1 { mode(M) : color(M) } 1 :- M != 0.

% Count occurrences of each colour 1..9
count(1) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,1) }.
count(2) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,2) }.
count(3) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,3) }.
count(4) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,4) }.
count(5) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,5) }.
count(6) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,6) }.
count(7) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,7) }.
count(8) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,8) }.
count(9) = #count{ R,C : input(R,C,9) }.

% Enforce that the chosen colour is a colour of maximal frequency
% (no other colour has a strictly larger count)
:- not mode(M), count(C), count(M), count(C) > count(M).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output cells for the two pattern blocks
1 { output(R,C,Color) : M = Color } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), mode(M), first_block(R,C).
1 { output(R,C,Color) : M = Color } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), mode(M), second_block(R,C).

% Output for cells that are not in any block (they must be 0)
1 { output(R,C,Color) : Color = 0 } 1 :- output_cell(R,C), not block1(R,C), not block2(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Basic output cell definition
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.
